concepts.title: Types of Debentures
concepts.description: There are mainly two types of debentures: Fixed Debentures and Floating Debentures. Fixed Debentures have a fixed rate of interest and a fixed date of repayment, while Floating Debentures have a floating rate of interest.

concepts.title: Debenture Trustee
concepts.description: A debenture trustee is a person or a body corporate appointed by the company to hold the debenture stock and to supervise the application of the debenture moneys.

concepts.title: Issue of Debentures
concepts.description: The issue of debentures is a process by which a company raises long-term funds by issuing debentures to the public, through a prospectus, or to a select group of individuals or institutions.

concepts.title: Debenture Capital vs Share Capital
concepts.description: Mnemonics: 'DEFEND' - Debenture capital is not a source of equity capital, it's a debt. It's 'D'ebenture, 'E'quity is not the same. 'F'ixed return is assured. 'E'quity holders bear risk. 'N'ot a source of permanent capital. 'D'ebenture holders have a fixed claim on the assets of the company.

concepts.title: Redemption of Debentures
concepts.description: Students often confuse debenture redemption with dividend payment. Redemption of debentures involves the repayment of the face value of debentures along with interest, whereas dividend payment involves the distribution of profits to the shareholders.

concepts.title: Debenture Features
concepts.description: Key features of debentures include: Face value, Maturity value, Rate of interest, Repayment terms, Debenture holder's rights, and Voting rights.

concepts.title: Conversion of Debentures
concepts.description: Conversion of debentures refers to the option given to debenture holders to convert their debentures into equity shares at a predetermined price and ratio, usually at the time of redemption.

concepts.title: Debenture Redemption Reserve
concepts.description: A debenture redemption reserve is a fund created by a company to meet the liability of redeeming debentures. It is created by setting aside a portion of the profits made by the company.
